company: Global X S&P 500 Index Corporate Class ETF
overview: The Global X S&P 500 Index Corporate Class ETF (HXS.TO), formerly Horizons S&P 500 Index ETF, tracks the S&P 500 using a swap-based (total return) structure. Unlike traditional ETFs, HXS does NOT pay distributions — instead, all returns (including dividends) are reflected in the share price through the swap. This makes it highly tax-efficient in non-registered accounts, as investors defer taxes until they sell. HXS is CAD-hedged, meaning it neutralizes USD/CAD currency fluctuations. MER is 0.10%. It is the preferred S&P 500 ETF for tax-conscious Canadian investors holding in taxable accounts.
bullCase: Swap-based structure pays no distributions — extremely tax-efficient in non-registered accounts.
CAD-hedged, eliminating USD/CAD currency risk for Canadian investors.
Low MER of 0.10% for S&P 500 exposure with built-in hedging.
bearCase: Swap counterparty risk — relies on a bank swap agreement rather than holding stocks directly.
CAD-hedged means no benefit when USD strengthens (unlike unhedged VFV).
No distributions means no regular cash income — unsuitable for income-seeking investors.
Tax-efficiency advantage only applies in non-registered accounts; no benefit in RRSP/TFSA.
analystSummary: HXS is the tax-efficiency king for S&P 500 exposure in taxable Canadian accounts. Swap-based, CAD-hedged, 0.10% MER, zero distributions. Not ideal for registered accounts where VFV or ZSP may be better.
seoDescription: Explore if HXS is right for you — Global X S&P 500 Index ETF, swap-based and CAD-hedged at 0.10% MER with zero distributions.
